meaning: adj.肆无忌惮的,无道德的,不谨慎的
examples: These kids are being exploited by very unscrupulous people.
The new law will be a charter for unscrupulous financial advisers.
When he is without virtue, man is the most unscrupulous and savage of the animals.
"It can, of course, be stolen or acquired by more unscrupulous methods", he added.
To secure that valuable real estate, unscrupulous vending operators will offer commissions up to 40%.
